name: Upcycling Hang, Weave and Remake
description:
Image Credits: Simi Mary
Turn your waste materials into something beautiful!

In this weaving workshop, participants learn the basics of weaving using different types of looms, including circular looms, frame looms, and branch weaving. Most materials are sourced from waste cloth, encouraging sustainability and creative reuse, while wool and threads are used to explore varied textures and patterns.

Participants are also invited to bring their own waste fabric to integrate into their work. 

This event is held in conjunction with our exhibition, mild tooth of milk, currently on display at the Dutch Warehouse in Fort Kochi.

name: Patchwork Cat Studio
description:
Create your own playful patchwork cat with artist Vasundhara W in this fun, hands-on workshop inspired by geometric shapes and fabric craft. Using tangram-style forms, colourful fabric, and simple stitching techniques, participants will design amusing cats in a variety of poses.

By the end of the session, you’ll have a fun patchwork cat placemat to take home.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Curator’s POV
description:
How has the most meme’able, memorable and mischievous animal known to man been represented across art history in the subcontinent?

Join curator Khushi Bansal as she explores the many lives of the Indian cat—appearing in paintings, sculptures, matchbox covers, artist sketchbooks, and beyond.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Singing in the Dark
description:
Image credits: Arati Kumar-Rao
Join us for a conversation by Jeet Thayil moderated by Joshua Muyiwa and Samyuktha Varma. Singing in the Dark: Art Against Genocide brings forth conversations that make us think, make us question and make us human. We ponder, or rather assert, the role of art, as resistance, as hope, as resilience during times of crisis. Thayil’s and Muyiwa’s poetry enriches the conversation as Varma adds depth to our thoughts.

 

This is in collaboration with Ananya Drishya.

name: Textile Conversations
description:
Image Credits: Poornima Jain-Haveli Textile Collection
Textile Conversations is designed as a two-part engagement programme exploring material knowledge in the Haveli Collection through dialogue and hands-on practice.

Through knowledge exchange between collaborators, each session positions textile researchers and practitioners as contributing distinct forms of expertise - technical, functional, embodied and aesthetic.

The programme draws strongly from the Haveli Collection at MAP, that consists of over 2741 textile objects and over 2050 archival materials, all belonging to the Haveli fashion brand established in the 1980s by Poornima Jain in Maharani Bagh, Delhi.

name: In the Company of Cats
description:
Image Credits: Sanchita Jain
Join us for a lively conversation about cats, their histories, myths, and personalities. Exploring two books on Indian cats, B. N. Goswamy's The Indian Cat and Olliewanders, you can swap stories, challenge common misconceptions, and celebrate the surprising complexity of our feline companions.

Expect good conversation, plenty of colour, and a "mosaic of many cats" who may look suspiciously like their humans, especially for anyone who suspects they might, spiritually, be a cat.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: the power of puppet companions
description:
This workshop invites participants to explore touch, imagination and feminist storytelling through making. Surrounded by Eisma’s playful and tactile world, participants will create their own hand puppets using socks, fabric, and simple craft materials.

We invite you to bring your stories on themes of care, transformation, and the power of gentle voices. Which have been important creatures, human or not, that have held you when you have needed it? 

Drawing from feminist perspectives that value vulnerability, creativity, and shared experience, we encourage you to give shape and colour to these stories and characters, and let the sock become puppet characters, messengers of small, personal, and collective stories that are there to speak and listen, resist or comfort us.

This event is held in conjunction with our exhibition, mild tooth of milk, currently on display at the Dutch Warehouse in Fort Kochi.

name: Meet the Artist- afra eisma
description:
Join afra eisma for an intimate artist interaction at mild tooth of milk. This gathering offers a rare opportunity to step into eisma’s vibrant, tactile world, where themes of the body, emotion, and transformation unfold through soft sculptures and immersive forms. Expect an engaging conversation about her practice and creative process, along with a chance to connect, ask questions, and experience the atmosphere up close.

Timings

Saturday, March 28, 11:00AM- 11:30AM

Saturday, March 28, 3:00PM- 3:30PM

Sunday, March 29, 11:00AM- 11:30AM

Sunday, March 29, 3:00PM- 3:30PM

This event is held in conjunction with our exhibition, mild tooth of milk, currently on display at the Dutch Warehouse in Fort Kochi.

name: Small Worlds, Many Cats
description:
Image credits: Mahaveer Swami
Learn traditional miniature techniques with artist Mahaveer, using the cat as your muse while exploring line, detail, and colour.

This hands-on workshop brings together storytelling, craftsmanship, and the feline’s enduring charm in Indian art.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Write from Art, Write from Story
description:
Explore the layered presence of the cat as muse, metaphor, and companion through a creative writing workshop led by writer and editor Indira Chandrasekhar.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.
